findings.line: Not yet whatever waits at the end of this, but far enough gone.
findings.reason: KILL - direct contrast pivot; the committed state survives without the negative runway.

findings.line: Not at the truth itself. At finally having words that matched his own warning.
findings.deletionTest: At finally having words that matched his own warning.
findings.reason: KILL - fragment pivot; the real cause lands cleanly without the negated preface.
findings.suggestedFix: At finally having words that matched his own warning.

audits.dominantPattern: scenery and abstractions carrying human judgment or agency

findings.subject: Daylight
findings.realAgent: the morning light exposing the wreckage and damage in visible detail
findings.reason: KILL - the sentence launders judgment onto daylight instead of naming the visible evidence.
findings.suggestedFix: In daylight, the harbor lay bare.

findings.line: The word he needed no name.
findings.subject: word
findings.realAgent: the people on the rise, who already understood that Cyndia meant Arthas
findings.reason: KILL - the noun is given a need that belongs to the people reading it.

findings.line: The land above the harbor had forgotten softness.
findings.subject: land
findings.realAgent: the hard crust, stone, and old ice under the drifts
findings.reason: KILL - scenery is given memory instead of the prose naming the hard ground directly.
findings.suggestedFix: The land above the harbor had gone hard.

findings.subject: Northrend
findings.realAgent: the climate, wind, and terrain that made the cold inescapable
findings.reason: KILL - geography is carrying command authority that belongs to the physical conditions.
findings.suggestedFix: In Northrend, the cold was absolute.

findings.line: Dwarven work announced itself even under snow.
findings.subject: work
findings.realAgent: the iron-banded trunks and unmistakable dwarven construction choices
findings.reason: KILL - the build is treated as self-presenting instead of being read through visible craft details.
findings.suggestedFix: Even under snow, the build was plainly dwarven.

findings.subject: Silence
findings.realAgent: the men on the wall who stopped answering after hearing Arthas's name
findings.reason: KILL - absence is given an active verb instead of the scene naming the men's reaction.

findings.line: relief and suspicion refusing to give ground
findings.subject: relief and suspicion
findings.realAgent: the guards holding both emotions on their faces at once
findings.reason: KILL - the abstractions are made to resist each other instead of the prose describing the men's expressions.
findings.suggestedFix: relief and suspicion set hard together on their faces

findings.line: Then his eyes went to Sylvanas, the rangers, the order in which they had entered and spread.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: Muradin assessing Sylvanas, the rangers, and their formation order
findings.reason: KILL - the body part steals agency from the character's tactical assessment.
findings.suggestedFix: Then he took in Sylvanas, the rangers, and the order in which they had entered and spread.

findings.line: His eyes found Jaina first. Then Sylvanas.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: Arthas choosing his first two points of focus
findings.reason: KILL - the body-part subject replaces the character's own act of looking.
findings.suggestedFix: He looked to Jaina first, then Sylvanas.

findings.line: Sylvanas looked back once toward the glacier and found, to her own surprise, that blame still refused to rise.
findings.subject: blame
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas, who cannot find blame in herself after the defeat
findings.reason: KILL - the abstraction is made to decide instead of the POV naming her actual state.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas looked back once toward the glacier and found, to her own surprise, only the hard blank where blame should have been.

findings.line: The recognition went down hard and steadied her.
findings.subject: recognition
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas taking the truth hard and regaining steadiness from it
findings.reason: KILL - the mental act is packaged as an independent force acting on her.
findings.suggestedFix: She took the truth hard and steadied.

findings.subject: word
findings.realAgent: Jaina, who is still catching up to what Sylvanas has just said
findings.reason: KILL - the noun is given a need that belongs to the listener.
KILL - this is one of the weakest generic gaze beats beyond the chapter cap.

findings.line: Jaina gave her a quick, startled look
findings.reason: KILL - this is another generic reaction-through-looking beat after the chapter has already exhausted the eye repertoire.

summary.verdictReason: Fail: pass A found 16 mild KILLs across all three guides; pass logic fails any run with more than 3 mild-only KILLs.
